question: Free vortex flow. In this type of flow, the liquid particles describe circular paths about a fixed vertical axis, without any external torque acting on the particles. The flow of water through the hole in the bottom of a wash basin is an example of free vortex flow. The following important points may be noted for vortex flow:

options.a: Reynold’s number. It is the ratio of the inertia force to the viscous force.
options.b: Froude’s number. It is the ratio of the inertia force to the gravity force.
options.c: Weber’s number. It is the ratio of the inertia force to the surface tension force.
options.d: Euler’s number. It is the ratio of the inertia force to the pressure force.
